nodes.x: Then Job answered and said,
Then Job answered and said,
But Job answered and said,
And Job answered and said,
Then Job answered the Lord, and said,
¶ Then Job answered the Lord, and said,
How long will ye vex my soul, and break me in pieces with words?
My soul is also sore vexed: but thou, O Lord, how long?
How long will it be ere ye make an end of words? mark, and afterwards we will speak.
How long shall I take counsel in my soul, having sorrow in my heart daily? how long shall mine enemy be exalted over me?
For I long to see you, that I may impart unto you some spiritual gift, to the end ye may be established;
After the number of the days in which ye searched the land, even forty days, each day for a year, shall ye bear your iniquities, even forty years, and ye shall know my breach of promise.
These ten times have ye reproached me: ye are not ashamed that ye make yourselves strange to me.
Yea, let none that wait on thee be ashamed: let them be ashamed which transgress without cause.
Let me not be ashamed, O Lord; for I have called upon thee: let the wicked be ashamed, and let them be silent in the grave.
I have stuck unto thy testimonies: O Lord, put me not to shame.
Let them be ashamed and brought to confusion together that rejoice at mine hurt: let them be clothed with shame and dishonour that magnify themselves against me.

11. Purge ye out the iniquity which is among you; sanctify
    yourselves before me,
And be it indeed that I have erred, mine error remaineth with myself.

1 Error after error must be cleared away, and proper view after view given, until a i thorough understanding is obtained and necessary steps are taken to bring about the | desired state of things. Besides this, it is also necessary, for the successful issue of i any movement, that all concerned should be worked into its spirit, and given to drink deeply of its fervour.
And now, my son, I desire that ye should labor diligently, that this gross error should be removed from among you; for, for this intent I have written this epistle.

do hope and pray my brethren and sisters to pay attention, that the Spirit of the Lord may be in your hearts, that you may see and understand things as they are. I would say, still further, if there be error advanced here, do not receive it, pass it by, and live so that you will know truth from error, light from darkness, the things that are of God from those not of God; and if an error should…
If indeed ye will magnify yourselves against me, and plead against me my reproach:
Thus will I magnify myself, and sanctify myself; and I will be known in the eyes of many nations, and they shall know that I am the Lord.
O magnify the Lord with me, and let us exalt his name together.
For I speak to you Gentiles, inasmuch as I am the apostle of the Gentiles, I magnify mine office:
Remember, O Lord, what is come upon us: consider, and behold our reproach.
Know now that God hath overthrown me, and hath compassed me with his net.
They compassed me about; yea, they compassed me about: but in the name of the Lord I will destroy them.
And it came to pass that after they had bound me insomuch that I could not move, the compass, which had been prepared of the Lord, did cease to work.
Thus saith the Lord God; I will therefore spread out my net over thee with a company of many people; and they shall bring thee up in my net.
And also Satan hath sought to deceive you, that he might overthrow you.
And now I have spoken the words which the Lord God hath commanded me.
Behold, I cry out of wrong, but I am not heard: I cry aloud, but there is no judgment.
Thou hast heard my voice: hide not thine ear at my breathing, at my cry.
I cry unto thee, and thou dost not hear me: I stand up, and thou regardest me not.
And said, I cried by reason of mine affliction unto the Lord, and he heard me; out of the belly of hell cried I, and thou heardest my voice.
Yea, thou art merciful unto thy children when they cry unto thee, to be heard of thee and not of men, and thou wilt hear them.
He hath fenced up my way that I cannot pass, and he hath set darkness in my paths.
He hath led me, and brought me into darkness, but not into light.
He hath set me in dark places, as they that be dead of old.
Because I was not cut off before the darkness, neither hath he covered the darkness from my face.
But now I go my way to him that sent me; and none of you asketh me, Whither goest thou?
He hath stripped me of my glory, and taken the crown from my head.

So I stood upon him, and slew him, because I was sure that he could not live after that he was fallen: and I took the crown that was upon his head, and the bracelet that was on his arm, and have brought them hither unto my lord.
He hath destroyed me on every side, and I am gone: and mine hope hath he removed like a tree.
He hath also broken my teeth with gravel stones, he hath covered me with ashes.
I was at ease, but he hath broken me asunder: he hath also taken me by my neck, and shaken me to pieces, and set me up for his mark.
He hath destroyed me on every side, and I am gone: and mine hope hath he removed like a tree.
He hath stripped me of my glory, and taken the crown from my head.
If I rejoiced at the destruction of him that hated me, or lifted up myself when evil found him:
He hath turned aside my ways, and pulled me in pieces: he hath made me desolate.

And the Spirit said unto me again: Behold the Lord hath delivered him into thy hands. Yea, and I also knew that he had sought to take away mine own life; yea, and he would not hearken unto the commandments of the Lord; and he also had taken away our property.
He hath also kindled his wrath against me, and he counteth me unto him as one of his enemies.
Behold, he findeth occasions against me, he counteth me for his enemy,
And it came to pass, when his master heard the words of his wife, which she spake unto him, saying, After this manner did thy servant to me; that his wrath was kindled.
And in nothing doth man offend God, or against none is his wrath kindled, save those who confess not his hand in all things, and obey not his commandments.
He teareth me in his wrath, who hateth me: he gnasheth upon me with his teeth; mine enemy sharpeneth his eyes upon me.
Therefore was the wrath of the Lord kindled against his people, insomuch that he abhorred his own inheritance.
His troops come together, and raise up their way against me, and encamp round about my tabernacle.
Knowing that shortly I must put off this my tabernacle, even as our Lord Jesus Christ hath shewed me.
And there I will meet with the children of Israel, and the tabernacle shall be sanctified by my glory.

And I will encamp about mine house because of the army, because of him that passeth by, and because of him that returneth: and no oppressor shall pass through them any more: for now have I seen with mine eyes.
Yea, I think it meet, as long as I am in this tabernacle, to stir you up by putting you in remembrance;
And I will set my tabernacle among you: and my soul shall not abhor you.
He hath put my brethren far from me, and mine acquaintance are verily estranged from me.
Lover and friend hast thou put far from me, and mine acquaintance into darkness.
Thou hast put away mine acquaintance far from me; thou hast made me an abomination unto them: I am shut up, and I cannot come forth.
And when he cometh home, he calleth together his friends and neighbours, saying unto them, Rejoice with me; for I have found my sheep which was lost.
And he, supposing that I spake of the brethren of the church, and that I was truly that Laban whom I had slain, wherefore he did follow me.
My kinsfolk have failed, and my familiar friends have forgotten me.
My lovers and my friends stand aloof from my sore; and my kinsmen stand afar off.
In silence and sorrow I made my lone bed. While far from my prison my friends had retired.
But they, supposing him to have been in the company, went a day’s journey; and they sought him among their kinsfolk and acquaintance.
And when she hath found it, she calleth her friends and her neighbours together, saying, Rejoice with me; for I have found the piece which I had lost.
They that dwell in mine house, and my maids, count me for a stranger: I am an alien in their sight.
I am become a stranger unto my brethren, and an alien unto my mother’s children.
Our inheritance is turned to strangers, our houses to aliens.
And strangers shall stand and feed your flocks, and the sons of the alien shall be your plowmen and your vinedressers.
That they may keep thee from the strange woman, from the stranger which flattereth with her words.
I am a stranger and a sojourner with you: give me a possession of a buryingplace with you, that I may bury my dead out of my sight.
I called my servant, and he gave me no answer; I entreated him with my mouth.
And he answered, My lord, O king, my servant deceived me: for thy servant said, I will saddle me an ass, that I may ride thereon, and go to the king; because thy servant is lame.
Then he called his servant that ministered unto him, and said, Put now this woman out from me, and bolt the door after her.
And he called one of the servants, and asked what these things meant.
I called you servants for the world’s sake, and ye are their servants for my sake—
And he bowed himself, and said, What is thy servant, that thou shouldest look upon such a dead dog as I am?
My breath is strange to my wife, though I entreated for the children’s sake of mine own body.
All the while my breath is in me, and the spirit of God is in my nostrils;

When the breath leaves the body, your life has not become extinct;
your life is still in existence. And when you are in the spirit world,
everything there will appear as natural as things now do. Spirits
will be familiar with spirits in the spirit world--will converse,
behold, and exercise every variety of communication with one another as
familiarly and naturally as while here in tabernacles.…
My breath is corrupt, my days are extinct, the graves are ready for me.
Yea, young children despised me; I arose, and they spake against me.
When I was a child, I spake as a child, I understood as a child, I thought as a child: but when I became a man, I put away childish things.
Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth: yet they have not prevailed against me.

And I contended with them, and cursed them, and smote certain of them, and plucked off their hair, and made them swear by God, saying, Ye shall not give your daughters unto their sons, nor take their daughters unto your sons, or for yourselves.

For the children of Israel and the children of Judah have only done evil before me from their youth: for the children of Israel have only provoked me to anger with the work of their hands, saith the Lord.
They would none of my counsel: they despised all my reproof.
All my inward friends abhorred me: and they whom I loved are turned against me.
They abhor me, they flee far from me, and spare not to spit in my face.
All that hate me whisper together against me: against me do they devise my hurt.
Consider mine enemies; for they are many; and they hate me with cruel hatred.

And it came to pass that they were sorrowful, because of their wickedness, insomuch that they did bow down before me, and did plead with me that I would forgive them of the thing that they had done against me.
I was a reproach among all mine enemies, but especially among my neighbours, and a fear to mine acquaintance: they that did see me without fled from me.
My bone cleaveth to my skin and to my flesh, and I am escaped with the skin of my teeth.
By reason of the voice of my groaning my bones cleave to my skin.
Wherefore do I take my flesh in my teeth, and put my life in mine hand?
My strength is dried up like a potsherd; and my tongue cleaveth to my jaws; and thou hast brought me into the dust of death.
My flesh is clothed with worms and clods of dust; my skin is broken, and become loathsome.
And it came to pass that I fell to the earth; and it was for the space of three days and three nights that I could not open my mouth, neither had I the use of my limbs.
Have pity upon me, have pity upon me, O ye my friends; for the hand of God hath touched me.
Therefore thus saith the Lord God; I have lifted up mine hand, Surely the heathen that are about you, they shall bear their shame.
For, behold, I will shake mine hand upon them, and they shall be a spoil to their servants: and ye shall know that the Lord of hosts hath sent me.

Touch me not, for God shall smite you if ye lay your hands upon me, for I have not delivered the message which the Lord sent me to deliver; neither have I told you that which ye requested that I should tell; therefore, God will not suffer that I shall be destroyed at this time.
For thus saith the Lord of hosts; After the glory hath he sent me unto the nations which spoiled you: for he that toucheth you toucheth the apple of his eye.

My friends, I bid you all adieu;
    The Lord hath called, and I must go--
    And all the joys of this vain earth,
    Are now to me of little worth:
    'Twill be the same with you as me,
    When brought as near eternity.
Why do ye persecute me as God, and are not satisfied with my flesh?
All thy commandments are faithful: they persecute me wrongfully; help thou me.
Behold, I said unto them: How is it that ye do not keep the commandments of the Lord? How is it that ye will perish, because of the hardness of your hearts?
But put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make not provision for the flesh, to fulfil the lusts thereof.

And why should I yield to sin, because of my flesh? Yea, why should I give way to temptations, that the evil one have place in my heart to destroy my peace and afflict my soul? Why am I angry because of mine enemy?
Lord, why castest thou off my soul? why hidest thou thy face from me?
Oh that my words were now written! oh that they were printed in a book!
Then Baruch answered them, He pronounced all these words unto me with his mouth, and I wrote them with ink in the book.

And if they are not the words of Christ, judge ye—for Christ will show unto you, with power and great glory, that they are his words, at the last day; and you and I shall stand face to face before his bar; and ye shall know that I have been commanded of him to write these things, notwithstanding my weakness.
Then said I, Lo, I come: in the volume of the book it is written of me,
Then the word of the Lord came to me, saying,
I had many things to write, but I will not with ink and pen write unto thee:
That they were graven with an iron pen and lead in the rock for ever!
And when they looked, they saw that the stone was rolled away: for it was very great.
Then the Jews took up stones again to stone him.
¶ And they wrought onyx stones inclosed in ouches of gold, graven, as signets are graven, with the names of the children of Israel.
And the five pillars of it with their hooks: and he overlaid their chapiters and their fillets with gold: but their five sockets were of brass.
For I know that my redeemer liveth, and that he shall stand at the latter day upon the earth:
For in mine own due time will I come upon the earth in judgment, and my people shall be redeemed and shall reign with me on earth.
And whoso taketh upon him my name, and endureth to the end, the same shall be saved at the last day.
And though after my skin worms destroy this body, yet in my flesh shall I see God:

And they shall go forth, and look upon the carcases of the men that have transgressed against me: for their worm shall not die, neither shall their fire be quenched; and they shall be an abhorring unto all flesh.
And all flesh shall see the salvation of God.
And in that day the enmity of man, and the enmity of beasts, yea, the enmity of all flesh, shall cease from before my face.
Whom I shall see for myself, and mine eyes shall behold, and not another; though my reins be consumed within me.
But behold, verily, verily, I say unto you that mine eyes are upon you. I am in your midst and ye cannot see me;
Mine eyes shall be upon the faithful of the land, that they may dwell with me: he that walketh in a perfect way, he shall serve me.
But mine eyes are unto thee, O God the Lord: in thee is my trust; leave not my soul destitute.
Mine eye also shall see my desire on mine enemies, and mine ears shall hear my desire of the wicked that rise up against me.
But ye should say, Why persecute we him, seeing the root of the matter is found in me?
And I said, Who art thou, Lord? And he said, I am Jesus whom thou persecutest.
Why do ye persecute me as God, and are not satisfied with my flesh?
And he fell to the earth, and heard a voice saying unto him, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?
And I answered, Who art thou, Lord? And he said unto me, I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom thou persecutest.

Said unto them, Ye have brought this man unto me, as one that perverteth the people: and, behold, I, having examined him before you, have found no fault in this man touching those things whereof ye accuse him:
Be ye afraid of the sword: for wrath bringeth the punishments of the sword, that ye may know there is a judgment.
Ye have feared the sword; and I will bring a sword upon you, saith the Lord God.

Then it shall come to pass, that the sword, which ye feared, shall overtake you there in the land of Egypt, and the famine, whereof ye were afraid, shall follow close after you there in Egypt; and there ye shall die.
For my sword shall be bathed in heaven: behold, it shall come down upon Idumea, and upon the people of my curse, to judgment.
Ye shall fall by the sword; I will judge you in the border of Israel; and ye shall know that I am the Lord.

O Lord, do not suffer that this people shall be destroyed by the sword; but O Lord, rather let there be a famine in the land, to stir them up in remembrance of the Lord their God, and perhaps they will repent and turn unto thee.
